questions.question: Explain the three main types of Machine Learning with one example of each.
keyPoints: Supervised Learning: learns from labeled data (e.g., email spam classification)
Unsupervised Learning: finds patterns in unlabeled data (e.g., customer segmentation)
Reinforcement Learning: learns through rewards/penalties (e.g., game-playing AI)

questions.question: What is overfitting? How can it be prevented?
keyPoints: Overfitting: model learns training data including noise
Prevention techniques: cross-validation, regularization, dropout, early stopping, reducing model complexity

questions.question: Differentiate between classification and regression problems in ML.
keyPoints: Classification: predicting discrete categories (e.g., spam/not spam)
Regression: predicting continuous values (e.g., house prices)
Output type and evaluation metrics differ between them

questions.question: What is the purpose of feature scaling in Machine Learning?
keyPoints: Normalizes feature ranges to similar scales
Prevents features with larger values dominating
Improves convergence speed in gradient-based algorithms
Common methods: Standardization, Min-Max Normalization

questions.question: Explain the concept of transfer learning and its advantages.
keyPoints: Uses pre-trained models on new but related tasks
Advantages: reduces training time, requires less data, improves performance
Example: using ImageNet pre-trained model for medical imaging

questions.question: What are the key evaluation metrics for classification problems?
keyPoints: Accuracy: overall correct predictions
Precision: correct positive predictions out of all positives predicted
Recall: actual positives correctly identified
F1-Score: harmonic mean of precision and recall

questions.question: Explain the bias-variance tradeoff in Machine Learning.
keyPoints: High bias: model too simple, underfits (misses patterns)
High variance: model too complex, overfits (learns noise)
Optimal model balances both for good generalization
Solution: cross-validation, regularization, proper model selection

questions.question: Discuss the applications of Machine Learning in healthcare domain with specific examples.
keyPoints: Medical Imaging: CNN for X-ray, MRI analysis, tumor detection
Disease Diagnosis: predictive models for diabetes, heart disease
Drug Discovery: ML for molecular analysis and compound prediction
Personalized Treatment: recommendation systems for patient care
Electronic Health Records: risk prediction and patient stratification
Benefits: early detection, improved accuracy, reduced costs

questions.question: Explain the working of a neural network and discuss its applications in computer vision.
keyPoints: Architecture: Input layer, hidden layers, output layer with neurons and weights
Forward propagation: data flows through layers with activation functions
Backpropagation: weights updated using gradient descent to minimize loss
Computer Vision applications: image classification (CNN), object detection, face recognition
Medical imaging analysis, autonomous vehicles, facial recognition systems

questions.question: What is Natural Language Processing? Discuss its applications and challenges.
keyPoints: NLP enables computers to understand human language
Applications: machine translation, sentiment analysis, chatbots, text summarization
Challenges: ambiguity, context understanding, sarcasm detection, multilingual processing
Techniques: tokenization, embeddings, transformers (BERT, GPT)

questions.question: Explain the concept of recommendation systems. Discuss collaborative filtering and content-based filtering approaches.
keyPoints: Recommendation Systems: predict user preferences for personalized suggestions
Collaborative Filtering: recommends based on similar users' preferences
Content-Based Filtering: recommends based on item features matching user history
Hybrid Systems: combine both approaches for better recommendations
Applications: Netflix, Amazon, Spotify, social media
